name: Rajgad Fort
description: Once the capital of the Maratha Empire, known for its sheer scale and the perilous Suvela Machi.

name: Torna Fort
description: The first fort captured by Shivaji Maharaj. It features steep climbs and magnificent views.

name: Sinhagad Fort
description: A popular fort near Pune with immense historical significance and a relatively easy trek.

name: Lohagad Fort
description: An excellent fort for beginners with wide pathways and the famous Vinchu Kata.

name: Visapur Fort
description: Located adjacent to Lohagad, it is famous for its waterfalls and large plateau during monsoons.

name: Raigad Fort
description: The capital of the Maratha Empire under Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj. Accessible via a ropeway.

name: Pratapgad Fort
description: Site of the famous Battle of Pratapgad. It has thick surrounding forests and a prominent watchtower.

name: Harishchandragad
description: Known for the majestic Konkan Kada, a massive cliff face, and the ancient Kedareshwar Cave.

name: Kalsubai Peak
description: The highest peak in Maharashtra. Features iron ladders to aid the steep ascent.

name: Ratangad Fort
description: Known as the 'Jewel of the Sahyadris'. Famous for its natural rock hole called 'Nedhe'.

name: Shivneri Fort
description: The birthplace of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj. It has seven gates and strong fortifications.

name: Tikona Fort
description: A triangular fort near Kamshet offering splendid views of the Pawna lake.

name: Korigad Fort
description: A popular monsoon trek in Lonavala. Has multiple lakes on the vast top plateau.

name: Rajmachi Fort
description: Consists of two twin fortresses: Shrivardhan and Manaranjan. A very popular monsoon destination.

name: Sudhagad Fort
description: Offers expansive views and features a large plateau. Was once considered for the Maratha capital.

name: Murud-Janjira
description: An incredible marine fort situated on an island. Famous for remaining unconquered.

name: Sindhudurg Fort
description: A massive sea fort built by Shivaji Maharaj. You can find his hand and foot prints preserved here.

name: Vijaydurg Fort
description: One of the oldest sea forts in Maharashtra, serving as an important naval base.

name: Karnala Fort
description: Located within a bird sanctuary, famous for its distinct pinnacle structure.

name: Panhala Fort
description: A large fort with historical significance, intricately linked to the Battle of Pavan Khind.

name: Vishalgad Fort
description: The destination fort where Shivaji Maharaj safely retreated after the battle of Pavan Khind.

name: Purandar Fort
description: Historically significant for the Treaty of Purandar. Birthplace of Chhatrapati Sambhaji Maharaj.

name: Vasota Fort
description: Situated amidst the dense Koyna Wildlife Sanctuary. Reached via a scenic boat ride.

name: Sajjangad Fort
description: A popular pilgrimage site as it holds the final resting place of Samarth Ramdas Swami.

name: Gorakhgad Fort
description: Known for its extremely steep, rock-cut steps. A thrilling climb for experienced trekkers.

name: Prabalgad Fort
description: Shares a plateau with Kalavantin Durg. Dense forest covers the vast summit.

name: Kalavantin Durg
description: World-famous for its terrifying, extremely steep, exposed rock-cut steps without railings.

name: Harihar Fort
description: Famous for its near-vertical rock-cut staircase at an 80-degree incline.

name: Salher Fort
description: The highest fort in the Sahyadri range and the second-highest peak in Maharashtra.

name: Dhodap Fort
description: The second highest fort in Maharashtra featuring an impressive rock-cut tunnel.
